There is no evidence of overlapping location, employer, or specific field of expertise.

results.possibleIdentity: Indiran Amarasingham was an electronics engineer active during the late 1960s and 1970s, primarily known for his work in solid-state circuit design and his technical collaborations with artists. During the late 1960s, he was employed as an electronics engineer at the Laboratory for Electronics (L.F.E.), which was located at 1079 Commonwealth Avenue in Boston, Massachusetts. He was an active member of the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) organization, where he was listed as a technical contact for artists seeking engineering assistance in the Boston area. Most notably, he served as a technical collaborator for the experimental filmmaker and video artist Amy Greenfield, assisting with the technological requirements of her dance-films and video works (such as those featured at the Anthology Film Archives). By the late 1970s, he was associated with Hipotronics Inc., a company based in Brewster, New York, that specialized in high-voltage test equipment.
